sections.paragraph:
to be cooked, ripened.

(1) to be cooked with fire, Eze 24:5.

(2) ripened with the heat of the sun, as the harvest, Joel 4:13 (Syr. ܒܫܶܠ Ch. בְּשַׁל, Eth. በሰበ፡ to be cooked, to be ripened. In the same manner the sense to be cooked, and to be ripened, are joined in other verbs; as in Arab. طبخ, Pers. جختن and نحتن bukhten and pukhten, which is kindred to the Germ. backen [Engl. to bake], Gr. πέπτω, πέσσω, Lat. coquitur uva, vindemia, Virg. Georg. ii. 522, Germ. die Traube kocht).

Piel causat. of Kal No. 1, to cook, especially flesh, Exo 16:23 29:31 Num 11:8 other food, 2Ki 4:38 6:29 .

Pual pass. of Piel, Exo 12:9 Lev 6:21.

Hiphil causat. of Kal No. 2, to ripen, Gen 40:10 see under the word אֶשְׁכֹּל .

Derived nouns מְבַשְּׁלֹות and-

בשל

m. Exo 12:9, בְּשֵׁלָה f. Num 6:19, Something boiled, sodden.
